item.description: The energy shock already rippling through Asia is set to come for countries in Europe and Africa that also rely on imports from the Middle East.

item.title: Saudi Arabia’s Grand Vision Slams Into Financial Reality—and the Iran War
item.description: The crown prince’s futuristic agenda was plagued by budget shortfalls and impractical designs. War plunged it deeper into jeopardy.

item.title: Fight Over Feta Strains America’s Ties With Europe
item.description: The Trump administration is requiring countries in trade deals to let U.S. companies market their cheeses under the names everyone recognizes.

item.title: A Downed Airman, a Mountain Hideout and a High-Risk Rescue in Iran
item.description: As Iranians closed in on an injured airman, the U.S. mounted a high-stakes mission into enemy territory.

item.title: Opinion | A Daring Rescue Inside Iran
item.description: The mission is more evidence of U.S. military competence and bravery.

item.title: Opinion | I Give Up on These Defeatists
item.description: From ‘No Kings’ and Iran to data centers, too many Americans are fighting progress.

item.title: Opinion | Mexico’s Missing and the Morena Party
item.description: Sheinbaum’s initial steps to restore public confidence in the government fall short.

item.title: Opinion | Europe Looks Away From Troubling Signs in Ukraine
item.description: The Continent’s leaders, seeking a buffer against Russia, ignore perils to democracy in Kyiv.

item.title: Opinion | The United Nations Has Lost Its Credibility
item.description: Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva wants a stronger U.N., but a failed institution can’t protect the rule of law.

item.title: Trump Warns Iran He Could Strike ‘Every Power Plant,’ in WSJ Interview
item.description: The president said he would act on the threat if the country’s leaders don’t agree to reopen the Strait of Hormuz by Tuesday evening, ratcheting up pressure on Tehran.

item.title: How Elite American Forces Are Trained to Survive Behind Enemy Lines
item.description: Air Force crew learn to eat bugs, treat their wounds and evade capture before ever flying a mission.

item.title: Trump Ramps Up Threats After Second Airman Is Rescued
item.description: Plus, the ranks of the affluent grow, a stock-market haven is at risk, and a guy running for Congress keeps showing up at quinceañeras.

item.title: China Creates New Aviation Mystery With Offshore Warning Zones
item.description: Beijing has reserved swaths of airspace for 40 days, suggesting possible military activity ahead.

item.title: U.S. Forces Rescue Second American Aviator in Iran
item.description: All forces involved in the rescue are safely out of Iranian airspace, officials say.

item.title: Life and Death Aboard Ships Stranded Near the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Hundreds of seafarers are calling for help to get home as food and fresh water start to run low after more than a month in limbo.

item.title: Trump’s Claims of Victory Clash With Iran War’s Gritty Reality
item.description: The Pentagon says the U.S. is achieving its goals, but Iran is fighting on.

item.title: The U.S.-Europe Alliance Is Reaching a Breaking Point Over the Iran War
item.description: President Trump has expressed “disgust” with European allies for not joining the U.S.-Israeli war with Iran, and has mused with aides about pulling out of NATO.

item.description: Officials say hitting power plants and roadways is necessary to cripple Iran’s weapons programs.

item.description: Friday marked one of the most complex search-and-rescue efforts for the Air Force in decades.

item.title: This Engineer Wants to Make Computer Chips on the Moon
item.description: Atsuyoshi Koike, the public face of Japan’s effort to muscle back into the semiconductor industry it used to dominate, first needs to prove he can make them on earth.

item.title: The Iran War Is Making the American Economy More Dominant Than Ever
item.description: America’s role as a major oil-and-gas exporter tempts President Trump to walk away from the Strait of Hormuz and wield leverage over others.

item.title: Trump’s Mission Impossible for Allies: Reopening the Strait of Hormuz
item.description: European leaders say forcing the passageway open militarily is unrealistic and more likely to come about through international pressure.

item.title: Iran Uses Asymmetric Warfare to Inflict Pain From a Weakened Position
item.description: The shooting down of two American warplanes was the most serious example of Tehran’s ability to raise the cost of the war for Washington.

item.description: Two drones hit the compound in Saudi Arabia, sparking a fire that raged for hours.

item.title: Opinion | The North Korea Lesson for Iran
item.description: Diplomacy failed to stop Pyongyang from getting the bomb. Trump didn’t make the same mistake.

item.title: Opinion | The Russian People Want the Internet Back
item.description: Online freedom becomes a burning issue as the government shuts down popular discontent.

item.title: Opinion | Trump and Bismarck in the Same Sentence
item.description: With his Iran war, the U.S. president is creating chaos for the world and wider options for himself.

item.title: Trump Joked About Macron’s Wife. He Wasn’t Amused.
item.description: An exchange between the two leaders this week was the latest sign that trans-Atlantic ties are souring over the Iran war.
